note: I'm adding domain param support to trans tag, only touching InternationalizationExtension in ext.py

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both features genuinely require editing InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in ext.py - true file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposing sequential ownership instead: I'll implement domain support now (small, isolated changes: parse an optional domain="literal" token in parse(), thread a domain var through _make_node() to call dgettext/dngettext instead of gettext/ngettext when set). I'll keep my changes additive and localized so they shouldn't collide with your note/created/modified metadata parsing, which is a separate concern (parsing extra keyword-style tokens, storing on node.trans_metadata). I'll message DONE when finished; you can then layer your changes on top. If you're in an isolated copy of the repo, feel free to proceed in parallel on your own copy - just keep metadata parsing/storage separate from the domain token handling so a manual merge stays easy.

If you're in an isolated copy of the repo, feel free to proceed in parallel on your own copy - just keep metadata parsing/storage separate from the domain token handling so a manual merge stays easy.

content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Here are exact locations/content of my changes in current ext.py (line numbers pre-edit) so you can place yours around them without colliding: (1) NEW module-level funcs _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext inserted right after _make_new_npgettext, i.e. right before 'class InternationalizationExtension' at current line 245 - purely additive, insert your own helpers elsewhere (e.g. after mine) if needed. (2) In _install() (currently lines 269-285): I add 'dgettext = getattr(translations, "dgettext", None)' / 'dngettext = getattr(translations, "dngettext", None)' with fallback closures, and pass dgettext=dgettext, dngettext=dngettext into the self._install_callables(...) call - I'm only adding lines, not changing existing ones there. (3) _install_null() (287-313): additive block for dgettext/dngettext fallback (mirrors the existing pgettext/npgettext hasattr pattern), plus added kwargs on the self._install_callables(...) call. (4) _install_callables() signature+body (315-337): I append 'dgettext: Optional[Callable]=None, dngettext: Optional[Callable]=None' params, add wrapping under the newstyle branch, and add dgettext=dgettext, dngettext=dngettext to the environment.globals.update(...) call - additive only. (5) _uninstall() (339-341): I add "dgettext","dngettext" to the tuple of keys popped - single line change. (6) parse() (354-467): inside the param while-loop (366-401), right after the 'if token.value in variables: parser.fail(...)' check (~line 380) and BEFORE the 'if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":' branch (~383), I insert a self-contained 'if token.value == "domain": ... continue' block that consumes 'domain="literal"' and validates via nodes.Const string check, storing into a local  var (not added to  dict, so it behaves like trimmed/notrimmed re: comma rules - no comma required immediately around it). I don't touch the existing assign/trimmed/else branches. At the _make_node(...) call site (~455-462) I append  as a new trailing positional arg. (7) _make_node() (509-571): I add  as new trailing param, and I REWRITE the two blocks at ~530-543 (the 'singular only' and 'singular and plural' Call-construction blocks) to pick gettext/dgettext or ngettext/dngettext based on whether domain is set. I do NOT touch anything from line ~545 to the end (545-571) - that region (newstyle kwarg loop / oldstyle Mod-dict wrapping / final return) is untouched by me, so it's safe for your metadata block to live there right before 'return nodes.Output([node])'. For your elif in the param loop: please place it as a separate self-contained elif (not editing my domain elif), and for _make_node, please append your  param AFTER my  param in the signature and after  in the _make_node(...) call args, and add your logic in the 545-571 region. This should keep our diffs non-overlapping. Let me know if that works.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain support implementation complete: parse() detects domain=STRING (must be a string-literal Const, else TemplateAssertionError containing string/literal/constant; duplicate domain raises TemplateAssertionError containing domain/twice), stored separately from variables. _make_node() takes a trailing domain param and emits dgettext(domain, message) / dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) calls instead of gettext/ngettext when set. Added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helpers and wired dgettext/dngettext through _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall with sensible fallbacks when the translations object lacks them. All 830 existing tests pass; verified manually with plural/trimmed/variable combos and both error cases. Didn't touch metadata logic.

Added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helpers and wired dgettext/dngettext through _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall with sensible fallbacks when the translations object lacks them. All 830 existing tests pass; verified manually with plural/trimmed/variable combos and both error cases. Didn't touch metadata logic.
